# Pierce County — Research and Data Analyst (Finance & Enterprise Services)

## Role summary

Policy-facing data analyst embedded in the Research and Data Analysis team within Pierce County's Finance & Enterprise Services Department. Cross-department scope: behavioral health, public safety, budget/program decisions. Emphasis on both quantitative and qualitative methods, data storytelling, and communicating findings to executives, County Council, and community audiences.

## Department context

Finance & Enterprise Services delivers enterprise-wide services across all County departments: budgeting/performance management, fiscal services, IT, procurement, fleet, risk management. The Research and Data Analysis team feeds directly into policy development, program evaluation, and investment-level decisions.

## Tech stack mentioned

SQL, Python, R, GitHub, Socrata (open data portal), Power BI, Tableau

## Key framing signals

- "translating complex methods into accessible insights" — communication is a primary success criterion
- "data stewardship at every step" — accuracy/validity of data sources, quality control methodology
- "qualitative and quantitative methods" — mixed methods is explicit; not a pure statistics role
- "behavioral health and public safety" — primary subject areas; Simon's behavioral analysis work is relevant
- Presenting to Executive, County Council, community — high-visibility communication expected

## Why this fits Simon

- snotrac: transit equity analysis with ACS + GTFS + OSM; policy brief delivered to Snohomish County regional agencies; direct precedent for county-level policy research
- dada_science: longitudinal behavioral analysis (ARIMAX, two-way ANOVA, STL decomposition); direct analog to program evaluation in behavioral health
- statistical_uniqueness: demographic/Census analysis, data stewardship framing (re-identification risk is a data quality problem)
- glacier_prethicktor: peer-reviewed publication establishes research credibility above typical AA/two-year requirement
- UW Admin Aide: public institution; compliance, reporting, audit readiness
- Finance background: aligns with Finance & Enterprise Services framing

## Gaps / honest notes

- No Power BI or Tableau; matplotlib/Folium is the visualization stack
- R is familiar (basic); not primary working language
- No Socrata-specific experience; ACS/Census/GTFS data pipelines are analogous
- "Behavioral health and public safety" as subjects: methodological fit is strong; domain knowledge is general
